gates.title: Delivery machine is self-orienting and self-healing
gates.rationale: The ceremonies loop must decide what to do next without human input, heal SMART failures by splitting, and close sprints cleanly regardless of completion. This is the meta-capability that accelerates everything else. Current fragility: py_compile catches syntax errors but not broken imports — a missing dependency or bad import statement causes a ModuleNotFoundError at runtime, stalling the loop with no gate signal. Indicator now includes an import smoke test so broken imports surface before the loop attempts to run.

indicator.command: bash -c 'python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py || (echo "G1 FAIL: ceremonies.py has syntax error" && exit 1); python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/lib/orient.py || (echo "G1 FAIL: orient.py has syntax error" && exit 1); python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/lib/gates.py || (echo "G1 FAIL: gates.py has syntax error" && exit 1); PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "from scripts.ralph.lib import orient, gates" || (echo "G1 FAIL: broken import in orient or gates" && exit 1); echo "G1 PASS: ceremony scripts compile clean and imports resolve"'

gates.title: Living state docs answer 'where are we' without reading history
gates.rationale: Every agent that touches this repo must be able to orient in under 2 minutes by reading two files. Without this, every session starts with archaeology. Rewritten each sprint by the sync ceremony. Current fragility: if the sync ceremony is skipped or fails silently, agent.md drifts out of date and the next session starts with a stale map — without detection.

gates.title: Zero external registry references in Helm chart templates
gates.rationale: Sovereignty requires that no chart template ever hard-codes an external registry. Every image reference must use {{ .Values.global.imageRegistry }}. IMPORTANT: indicator updated from charts/*/templates/ to platform/charts/*/templates/ after monorepo restructure — the old path pointed at an empty directory and produced a vacuous PASS, providing no protection.

indicator.command: bash -c 'result=$(grep -rn "docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io" platform/charts/*/templates/ 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$result" ] && echo "G6 PASS: no external registries in templates" || (echo "G6 FAIL:" && echo "$result" && exit 1)'

gates.title: Contract validator enforces sovereignty invariants — test suite passes
gates.rationale: contract/validate.py is the machine-enforced sovereignty gate. It is the only mechanism that guarantees autarky.externalEgressBlocked=true before any cluster is provisioned. If the test suite breaks (valid.yaml rejected or invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml accepted), the sovereignty invariant is silently unenforced. Test corpus expanded in increment 31 (CONTRACT-001) to cover imageRegistry and storageClass invariants. Fragile because the corpus covers core invariants but not edge cases — a schema extension or validator refactor that inadvertently relaxes enforcement will still pass G7 if the existing fixtures still pass. The test suite is the only line of defence.

indicator.command: bash -c 'python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 || (echo "G7 FAIL: valid.yaml rejected by validator" && exit 1); python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 && (echo "G7 FAIL: invalid contract accepted — autarky invariant not enforced" && exit 1); echo "G7 PASS: contract validator enforces sovereignty invariants"'

_retired.title: Planning pipeline always has a pending increment to execute
_retired.retiredReason: Triggered 7 remediation andons across increments 11, 13, 15, 18, 19, 20, 21 — all with the same band-aid fix (manually add a pending stub). This is not an egg protecting a value; it is a metric exposing a ceremony bug. The structural fix (plan ceremony auto-primes a pending stub after creating an active sprint) is in sprint 22 as KAIZEN-007r. Once that lands, G5 is structurally moot. An egg that has triggered 7 identical band-aids has failed its purpose — the fix must be in the ceremony system, not in a gate.

_retired.title: Cluster contract is current, enforced, and machine-checked
_retired.retiredReason: Graduated to table stakes. The file_exists check on docs/governance/cluster-contract.md has been healthy for 10+ consecutive sprints. The monorepo restructure introduced contract/validate.py which provides actual machine enforcement — that is now tracked by G7. A governance document is not the right indicator when a real validator exists.

_retired.title: Mental model is coherent — one vocabulary, one hierarchy
_retired.retiredReason: Graduated to table stakes. Vocabulary migration (increments/phases) completed many sprints ago. The gate_passing check always passes and has never triggered a remediation sprint. The word 'phases' does not appear in new code and the hard-stop rule in agent.md enforces this at the implementation level. Retiring frees a slot for fragility that is real and current.
